name: trim-validation
description: Validate that removing PAGES, TERRAIN, and DOMAINS sections does not hurt performance. Tests bundled trim vs full baseline with section-targeted questions.

questions.question: Compare the complexity distribution across modules. Which module has the highest average complexity and which has the lowest?

assertions.check: Response references specific complexity bar notation (e.g., block characters like squares/bars) or numeric complexity averages per module

assertions.description: Names high and low complexity modules
assertions.check: Response names at least one high-complexity module and one low-complexity module with supporting data

questions.question: Looking at module-level complexity, which modules are simple utilities vs complex business logic? How can you tell?

assertions.description: Uses TERRAIN data to classify modules
assertions.check: Response classifies modules by complexity level using quantitative data (complexity scores, line counts, or heatmap notation) rather than guessing from names alone

questions.question: What user-facing pages does the Teacher Lunch Club app have? List them.

assertions.check: Response lists at least 4 specific page names or paths from the teacher-club section (e.g., menu, orders, profile, register, catering, spirit-night)

questions.question: How many feature domains does this codebase have? Name them and their relative sizes.

assertions.check: Response names at least 3 feature domains with approximate file counts or relative size comparisons

questions.question: Orders are failing intermittently during lunch rush. Where should I look first?

assertions.check: Response mentions the orders route and at least one downstream file (ordering-server.ts, ordering.ts, or authorize-net.ts)
Response mentions complexity score, line count, or hotspot ranking for the orders route

questions.question: What would be the blast radius of changing ordering-server.ts?

assertions.check: Response mentions ordering-server.ts affects more files than its direct import count suggests, OR mentions approximately 20-25 affected files
Response mentions amplification ratio, cascade depth, or the AMP flag for ordering-server.ts

questions.question: Plan a DataCandy loyalty integration for Teacher Lunch Club. What modules need to change and what's the risk?

assertions.check: Response mentions the orders route or order submission as an integration point
Response mentions high import count or cascade risk for at least one module
